mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing and supply capacity, which historically correlates with rising corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Over a rolling 3-year window, the average 20-day return following INDPRO increases (mom>0) is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p>0.05) or the rank IC between INDPRO change and next 20d return is less than 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over a three-month period indicates deterioration in the labor market, which reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points within a 3-month window
failure_condition: When the trigger condition fires, the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in both employment (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) provides a powerful signal of an imminent economic downturn, as it reflects weakness in both labor demand and manufacturing output, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change is negative and INDPRO month-over-month change is negative
failure_condition: If both conditions are met, the average forward 20-day return is positive or not significantly different from zero (p>0.05) in a one-tailed test, or the signal produces more than 50% false positives (positive returns).

mechanism_narrative: During a period of extremely accommodative monetary policy (federal funds rate near zero), a consistent increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals strengthening consumer and residential investment demand, which boosts economic growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a short 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases for two consecutive months
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle (zero interest rate policy)
failure_condition: The average 5-day return after two consecutive HOUST increases during the ZIRP period is not positive (mean return ≤ 0) or the win rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) reflects robust consumer spending, the primary driver of US GDP. Month-over-month expansion in RSAFS signals improving consumption momentum, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day trading window.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending accounts for ~70% of GDP
failure_condition: The rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month change and forward 5-day returns is less than 0.02 over any 5-year rolling window, or the mean return following positive RSAFS mom is not significantly greater than zero (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) to a level above 240 (index value) indicates building inflationary pressures, which prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy. Higher discount rates and reduced equity valuations lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: any regime but especially during expansion
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL > 240, the average 60-day forward return is not negative (mean >= 0) or the signal yields less than 55% negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or below 1% and industrial production is declining, it signals a liquidity trap where monetary policy cannot stimulate the economy. This combination produces deflationary concerns and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS <= 1% and INDPRO month-over-month change < 0 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: The average forward 20-day return following simultaneous trigger conditions is not negative (p>0.05), or the signal's predictive accuracy (directional hit rate) is below 50% over a historical period of zero lower bound.

mechanism_narrative: In a period of below-trend GDP growth, a strong payrolls report (PAYEMS adding more than 200,000 jobs in a month) signals that the labor market is gaining momentum, which supports consumer income and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Above-trend payrolls during slow growth signals recovery
failure_condition: During below-trend GDP growth (e.g., real GDP growth < 2% YoY), the average 60-day forward return after a >200k payrolls increase is not positive (mean <= 0) or the hit rate is below 55%.
